commands.review_summary:
The PR changes bundled session MCP catalog loading to validate `tools/list` entries individually, quarantine invalid tools with diagnostics, preserve SDK metadata caching for surviving tools, and add focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +134, Tests +109. Total +243 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection. Current main calls the SDK `client.listTools` path that validates the full `ListToolsResultSchema`, and the adjacent test shows an invalid tool schema currently leaves the catalog empty.
commands.review_followup: - Maintainer-authored draft with a protected `maintainer` label should remain in the normal maintainer review and validation lane, not automated cleanup or repair.

target.title: fix(agents): quarantine invalid bundle MCP tools
target.body:
## Summary
- Request bundled MCP `tools/list` with a permissive outer schema, then validate tools one by one.
- Quarantine invalid MCP tool entries and metadata-cache failures with catalog diagnostics while keeping healthy sibling tools available.
- Preserve SDK metadata caching for surviving tools so output-schema validation and task-support behavior remain intact.

## Verification
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts -t "records diagnostics when tools/list returns an invalid tool schema|skips invalid tools/list entries while keeping healthy siblings|skips tools whose output schema metadata cannot be cached|filters listed MCP tools with per-server include and exclude rules" --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: A bundled MCP server could return one invalid `tools/list` entry and cause OpenClaw to reject the whole list, dropping healthy sibling tools and reporting only a server-level startup diagnostic.

Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree on branch `fuzz-tool-schema-next66-20260603`, Node/Vitest through `scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; remote `check:changed` pending.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: See Verification above.

Evidence after fix: The pre-patch regression failed with `catalog.tools` equal to `[]` instead of `healthy_tool`. After the patch, focused MCP tests passed for invalid input-schema quarantine, invalid output-metadata quarantine, and normal include/exclude filtering. Full `agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts` passed: 1 file, 32 tests. Autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: Invalid MCP tools are skipped with diagnostics naming the offending tool; healthy sibling tools remain in the catalog and surviving tools are re-cached through the SDK metadata path.

What was not tested: Full repo suite and remote `pnpm check:changed` are not complete yet; this PR will be updated after Crabbox proof finishes.
target.author: vincentkoc
